Applicants can now pay USCIS fees by ACH debit. Simply complete and sign our new Form G-1650, Authorization for ACH Transactions, and place the form on top of your application, petition, or request when you file.

We will continue to accept paper checks, money orders, and credit and debit transactions until Oct. 28, 2025. Starting Oct. 28, we will accept only ACH debit transactions using Form G-1650 or credit card payments using Form G-1450, Authorization for Credit Card Transactions.

To learn more, visit https://t.co/EkRIPuk1tW

tweets.text:
"The U visa program is another example of a great idea gone terribly wrong through gross mismanagement and corruption. Envisioned as a law enforcement tool to protect bona fide alien crime victims, the U visa program is being exploited by fraudsters and criminal aliens, their attorneys, and corrupt law enforcement. The law allows aliens to qualify for U nonimmigrant status even if they entered the United States illegally, or have prior deportations, final orders of removal, and criminal convictions. Bad actors have every incentive to fabricate stories to get a U visa. USCIS is aware of several fraud and program integrity concerns commonly found within the U visa program, including: aliens staging crimes to obtain U visa law enforcement certifications, state and local law enforcement agencies “rubber stamping” U visa certifications, and aliens filing meritless U visa petitions to delay deportation and obtain employment authorization.

U visas are capped at 10,000 annually by statute (not including derivative relatives). From FY 2017 to FY 2025 (Q2) USCIS received 266,293 principal U visa petitions. This means that if all U visa filings stopped today, it would take over 20 years adjudicate the cases in the backlog.  Since FY 2009, USCIS has seen a 2,013% increase in pending cases.

Under the leadership of @POTUS and @Sec_Noem, USCIS has taken the handcuffs off our officers to pursue U visa fraudsters. This prosecution is just the tip of the iceberg." - @USCIS Spokesman Matthew J. Tragesser

Read more: https://t.co/EG3N72GJOR

This Independence Day, we celebrate a safer America thanks to the Trump administration’s commitment to safeguard the American people and uphold our values. Congratulations to all!

Under @POTUS and @Sec_Noem’s direction, USCIS will ensure that sufficient safeguards are in place to prevent any stateless alien from being admitted to the U.S. without undergoing stringent identity verification beyond that required of any other alien seeking admission or entry to the U.S.  https://www.uscis.gov/sites/default/files/document/policy-manual-updates/20250605-StatelessnessRescission.pdf
